# DESIGN.md — Per-Project Design Spec
# Generated by Claude from the project brief. Reviewed and approved before the work session starts.
# Claude reads this file at the start of every work session before writing any TSX.
# Update this file when the design direction evolves — do not version it, just edit in place.

---

## PROJECT
- **Name:** {{PROJECT_NAME}}
- **Output type:** {{OUTPUT_TYPE}}
- **Platform:** {{PLATFORM}}

---

## CREATIVE DIRECTION

### Feel
> 3 adjectives that describe the intended emotional response when someone sees this output.
> These are the north star — every design decision should reinforce at least one of them.

{{FEEL_ADJECTIVE_1}} · {{FEEL_ADJECTIVE_2}} · {{FEEL_ADJECTIVE_3}}

### Tone
> Where does this sit on these axes? Claude uses this to calibrate density, weight, and contrast.

- **Minimal ←————→ Rich:** {{MINIMAL_TO_RICH}}        (e.g. 3/10 = very minimal, 8/10 = layered and detailed)
- **Dark ←————→ Light:** {{DARK_TO_LIGHT}}             (e.g. 2/10 = near-black base, 9/10 = white base)
- **Technical ←————→ Human:** {{TECHNICAL_TO_HUMAN}}   (e.g. 2/10 = data/code aesthetic, 8/10 = warm and approachable)
- **Loud ←————→ Quiet:** {{LOUD_TO_QUIET}}             (e.g. 2/10 = bold and aggressive, 8/10 = restrained and elegant)

### Reference feel
> A known design, product, or aesthetic this should feel adjacent to — not copy, feel adjacent to.
> Examples: "Bloomberg Terminal meets Stripe Docs", "Notion-level whitespace with Linear's precision"

{{REFERENCE_FEEL}}

---

## COLOR

### Palette
- **Background:** `{{BG_COLOR}}`
- **Surface (cards, panels):** `{{SURFACE_COLOR}}`
- **Primary accent:** `{{PRIMARY_COLOR}}`
- **Secondary accent:** `{{SECONDARY_COLOR}}` *(optional — use sparingly)*
- **Primary text:** `{{TEXT_COLOR}}`
- **Muted text (captions, labels):** `{{TEXT_MUTED_COLOR}}`
- **Border / divider:** `{{BORDER_COLOR}}`

### Hue tinting
> Vercel technique: tint all non-neutral elements (borders, shadows, muted text) toward the primary accent hue.
> This creates coherence without adding more colors.

{{HUE_TINTING_DIRECTION}}
*(e.g. "Tint borders and muted text slightly toward cyan — never pure grey")*

### Usage rules
- Max 3 colors in active use per composition (background + accent + text)
- Secondary accent: highlight only — one element per composition maximum
- No decorative gradients unless they serve a structural purpose

---

## TYPOGRAPHY

### Scale
- **Display (hero headline):** {{DISPLAY_SIZE}}px · {{DISPLAY_WEIGHT}} · {{DISPLAY_TRACKING}}
- **Heading:** {{HEADING_SIZE}}px · {{HEADING_WEIGHT}}
- **Body:** {{BODY_SIZE}}px · {{BODY_WEIGHT}} · {{BODY_LINE_HEIGHT}} line height
- **Caption / label:** {{CAPTION_SIZE}}px · {{CAPTION_WEIGHT}} · {{CAPTION_TRANSFORM}}

### Hierarchy rule
> How many type sizes should appear in one composition, and what role each plays.

{{TYPOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY_RULE}}
*(e.g. "Max 3 sizes per composition: display anchors attention, heading organizes, caption supports")*

### Typographic character
> Beyond size — describe the personality. Tight and condensed? Open and airy? Uppercase labels?

{{TYPOGRAPHIC_CHARACTER}}

---

## SPATIAL RHYTHM

### Base unit
> All spacing derives from this. Multiples only — no arbitrary values.

Base: {{SPACING_BASE}}px → scale: ×2, ×4, ×8, ×16

### Density
> How much breathing room between elements. Guides padding, gap, and margin decisions.

{{SPATIAL_DENSITY}}
*(e.g. "Generous — minimum 32px between sections, 16px within components")*

### Composition zones
> How the canvas divides visually. Where should the eye land first, second, third?

{{COMPOSITION_ZONES}}
*(e.g. "Top third: headline + category signal. Middle: proof/asset. Bottom: outcome statement")*

---

## SURFACE & TEXTURE

### Background treatment
> Flat color, subtle noise, gradient, geometric pattern — or none?

{{BACKGROUND_TREATMENT}}

### Card / panel treatment
> How surfaces are distinguished from the background — border, elevation, or neither?

{{SURFACE_TREATMENT}}
*(e.g. "1px border at border color, no shadow — elevation via color not depth")*

### Decorative elements
> Abstract shapes, geometric accents, grid lines, noise texture — what's allowed and what's not?

{{DECORATIVE_ELEMENTS}}
*(e.g. "One subtle geometric accent per composition — must be abstract, never representational")*

---

## ANTI-PATTERNS
> What this project must NOT look like. Claude checks every composition against this list.

- {{ANTI_PATTERN_1}}
- {{ANTI_PATTERN_2}}
- {{ANTI_PATTERN_3}}

*(e.g. "Not a SaaS landing page hero", "No rainbow gradients", "No stock-photo aesthetic", "No centered everything")*

---

## COMPOSITION REFERENCE
> Once the first composition is approved, record its ID here so future compositions anchor to it.

- **Reference composition:** {{REFERENCE_COMPOSITION_ID}} *(e.g. MetaAdHero — approved 2026-04-02)*
- **What to match:** palette, spacing rhythm, typographic scale, surface treatment
- **What can vary:** layout structure, zone arrangement, decorative element placement
